question.text: The savings ratio is calculated as:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Savings Ratio = Savings per year / Annual Income. It measures what percentage of income a person is able to save. **The Trap:** Total Assets/Annual Income is the accumulated savings-to-income ratio, a different metric. Don't confuse the flow measure (savings ratio) with the stock measure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Personal finance ratios are core to X-A (Ch 3) and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A savings ratio of at least 20-25% is generally recommended — help clients track this monthly.
